task_name: Routes — read tools/budget, persist + replay trace

strengths: Cycle-1 Critical fix correctly applied: insertColumns now passes the raw JS array via `tools ?? []` (routes.ts:120) so node-pg serializes it to a Postgres TEXT[] literal ('{web_search}' / '{}'), matching the `tools TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'` column (agent-schema.sql:40). JSONB `input` in insertToolCall correctly keeps JSON.stringify (routes.ts:304).

Cycle-1 Minor fix correctly applied: two serialization tests (routes.test.ts:52-106) assert a raw array (not a JSON string) is passed for the $8 tools parameter, including the undefined→[] case. Tests drive the real path because realDeps.insertColumns delegates to the same runInsertColumns helper (routes.ts:179-180), so the assertions are not tautological.

Trace replay is genuinely read-only: getToolTrace issues exactly one SELECT against agent_tool_calls scoped by board_id ORDER BY created_at (routes.ts:57-63); the GET-board handler attaches the result as a flat board-level `toolTrace` array (routes.ts:543-545) and never invokes a tool — satisfies spec R5 and the LOCKED flat-shape contract.

getColumns extended to select and map tools/tool_budget into ColumnInfo (routes.ts:258-271), supporting R1 data-driven per-column gating; workspace ownership guard (requireWorkspaceMember) is intact on the GET route (routes.ts:499).
Full agent test suite green: 73 passed, 1 skipped — confirms the correction's refactor (extracting runInsertColumns, rewiring realDeps through it) introduced no test regressions.

issues.description: realDeps.insertColumns delegates via a type cast `data as Parameters<typeof runInsertColumns>[1]` (routes.ts:180). This is benign widening to Record<string, unknown> and typechecks clean, but a direct typed adapter would avoid the cast. Non-blocking.
